observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to instruct the GAD Focal Person to assess PPAs utilizing the methodologies outlined in the PWC-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC Circular No. 2013-01 and 2016-01. This will ensure that gender-related issues and concerns are effectively addressed.
observations.reason: (Reiterated in this year’s Annual Audit Report)

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our previous recommendation for Management to immediately adopt the use of ATM in the payment of salaries, wages, bonuses and other personnel benefits in order to:

Strengthen the internal control system in the Treasury Office,

Eliminate possible risk of theft or robbery during personal withdrawals in bank for payroll funds,

Reduce the workload and time spent in preparation of pay envelopes,

Avoid the release of salaries, wages, honorarium and other benefits to unauthorized individuals, and

Eliminate risk of human error in the distribution of pay envelopes.
observations.reason: (Reiterated in this year’s Annual Audit Report)

observations.recommendation:
1.13	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive take steps to transfer the buildings by formally requesting the SB to pass a resolution that authorizes the transfer of these assets to the designated recipient local government units and national agencies.
1.14	Furthermore, upon securing the SB resolution and necessary donation documents, the Municipal Accountant should prepare the appropriate adjusting entry to formally recognize the donation. To prevent an artificial inflation of expenses in the current year – which could distort financial performance and potentially result in an operating loss – the adjustment should not be recorded as a current-year expense. Instead, since these assets pertain to prior periods, they should have been classified as donations in previous years. We suggest closing the accounts under Prior Period Adjustments. The recommended adjusting entry is as follows:
Prior Period Adjustment				xxx
Accumulated Depreciation				xxx
Buildings 						xxx
School Buildings					xxx
1.15 	Lastly, to ensure comparability and consistency in financial reporting, the Municipal Accountant should prepare restated financial statements. These restated statements should accurately reflect the impact of the depreciation expenses recognized in previous years. This will enhance the reliability and transparency of financial information, enabling stakeholders to make well-informed comparisons across reporting periods and support financial decision-making.

In view of the foregoing observations and to strengthen the management, recording, and regulation of rent income from market stalls, we recommended the following measures:
1. The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should establish a well-documented and consistent coordination mechanism for the monitoring and recording of rent income from market operations. Specifically:
The Municipal Treasurer (MT) should provide a monthly list of delinquent operating leases receivables, clearly segregating regular lease receivables from penalties and surcharges due to late payments.
The Municipal Accountant (MA) should ensure that such transactions are recorded using the accrual basis of accounting, in accordance with government accounting standards, and using the appropriate journal entries as detailed in the body of this audit observation.
This will enhance the accuracy of financial reporting, provide a clearer picture of receivables, and support better revenue monitoring and collection efficiency.
2. The Municipality should constitute a Market Oversight Committee, composed of representatives from the Treasurer’s Office, Legal Office, Market Administration, and other relevant departments, tasked with the following:
Conduct an investigation into the reported cases of unauthorized subleasing of market stalls;
Regularly monitor market stall occupancy and usage, ensuring that only duly registered stallholders operate in their assigned spaces;
Enforce the provisions of lease contracts, including penalties or revocation clauses for violations such as subleasing;
Establish a monitoring database or system to keep track of stallholder information, payments, and stall utilization.
This initiative will promote transparency, prevent abuse of public property, and ensure that stalls serve their intended public purpose.
3. The Sangguniang Baya Sanggunian Should enact a resolution and corresponding ordinance to review and update the outdated annexes of the Municipal Revenue Code pertaining to the Market Operations, specifically those setting rental rates, penalties, and conditions of lease.
This update should align the rates with prevailing market values, taking into account inflation and economic conditions.
A revised rate structure will not only result in increased municipal revenue, but also act as a regulatory measure to discourage stallholders from subleasing stalls at a profit.
Reasonable increases in rental rates may also motivate legitimate tenants to fully utilize their stalls for commercial activities, thereby supporting local economic development and proper space utilization.
By implementing these recommendations, the Municipality can enhance its fiscal performance, improve market administration, and uphold accountability in the management of public assets.

4.19 	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
The Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the policy that no additional cash advance shall be granted unless the previous advance has been fully liquidated, in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
Accountable officers to liquidate cash advances for local travels within 30 days after returning to their official station.
If an accountable officer fails to attend a scheduled seminar, training, or official travel, the full cash advance must be returned immediately.
The Municipal Accountant to ensure that liquidation reports are completed accurately, including the liquidation date, which serves as the basis for audit verification and compliance with established deadlines.

5.13	 We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to undertake the following measures:
Conduct a thorough evaluation of the proposed or attributed PPAs to ensure they are genuinely responsive to identified gender-related issues and concerns;
Provide additional supporting documentation or justification to substantiate the mainstreamed PPAs; and
Regularly update the GAD Database to facilitate the accurate identification, monitoring, and analysis of gender issues.
These actions will enhance the integrity and effectiveness of the municipality's GAD initiatives, ensure proper classification of PPAs, and align with relevant policies and guidelines.

6.8 	We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management promptly implement the use of ATM to disburse salaries, wages, bonuses, and other personnel benefits for internal control purposes to achieve efficient and effective cash handling.

7.6 	We recommended that management, through the engineering department, plan its projects with precision, foresight, and skill to avoid unnecessary or excessive procurement of items.
We further recommend that utmost consideration in the immediate plans for PPAs of the LGU focuses on the utilization of the uninstalled solar panels to obtain economic benefit while still functional. Maximum benefits from all drafted projects must always be kept in mind while incurring the least cost possible.

8.11 	We recommended that management maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for various projects, programs, and activities aimed at achieving desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU, in compliance with Sections 3.2 to 3.2.4 of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 001 dated November 4, 2020.

We have reiterated our previous   
      recommendation that the  
      Municipal Mayor:

To create an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory in three months or less. The Inventory Committee shall have at least one member each from the Accounting and Property Divisions/Units of the agency.

To instruct the Inventory Committee/Treasury office and Accounting office to reconcile the differences, if any, between the physical inventory report and the general ledger balance of each PPE account; subsequently, the Accountant shall identify if the difference represents PPE that are still existing, no longer existing but with pertinent records, or vice-versa.

To apprise the conduct of annual physical inventory and make updates on the current/ existing inventory reports, and to adhere with the timeline of activities under the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP). The Inventory Committee and accounting shall compare the latest Report on the RPCPPE/Inventory Report with the PPE Ledger Cards (PPELCs);

To enjoin the Inventory Committee to prepare a list of PPE items which are recorded in the PPELCs but not included in the RPCPPE/Physical Inventory Report as well as PPE items which are included in the RPCPPE but not recorded in the PPELCs;

That after the completion of physical count, for the PPEs classified as non-existing/ missing and of those without available records of accountability, the procedures enumerated under Items 7 and 8 of the same COA Circular for the disposition and derecognition must be done.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:

The Municipal Assessor to trace the historical cost of the identified lot of the Municipality.

The Management to exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lot with tax declaration number 41759 through a TCT in the name of the Municipality.

The Municipal Assessor, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records pertaining to Land account. Determine the acquisition cost of each land parcel composing the Land account.

In cases where a parcel was acquired thru non-exchange transaction, such as donations, determine the prevailing fair value at the date of acquisition.

For the Municipal Accountant to record the following suggested journal/adjusting entries after the cost, FMV of the Land were identified:

To record the cost of Land based on traced acquisition cost (for purchased Land)/ FMV (for Land received through donation) from Assessor’s Office:
Land	xxx
Prior Year’s Adjustment    xxx	xxx

To record the capitalizable costs initially expensed in the books instead of recording it as part of Land Account:
Land (ex. Transfer Tax, Titling Expenses, Land Filling) xxx
Prior Year’s Adjustment  xxx	xxx

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate the distribution of the respective OR/CR of the donated vehicles to the barangays, as mandated by the Land Transportation Office (LTO) for the transfer of ownership. 

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to drop the recorded Rescue Vehicles from the accounts, and record the corresponding donation as follows:

Prior Year Adjustments 			₱47,251,840.00
					
Motor Vehicle
                      ₱47,251,840.00

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant also record the receipt of the donated vehicles in books of the component barangays.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:

The Municipal Accountant to record purchased inventory on their appropriate asset account, use perpetual inventory method and for subsequent valuation, use weighted average method. Also, the MA should maintain a Supply Ledger Card for each commodity/stock.


The Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of GSO, likewise maintain stock cards for supplies to account for the receipt and disposition of the same. The balance per stock card should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit.

Department Offices to use the RIS to record used inventory. This shall serve as monitoring of inventories requested and issued.

The Municipal Treasurer and the Supply Officer to coordinate with the Department Offices in the consolidation of RIS and to prepare a weekly SSMI.

Additionally, for the Municipal Accountant to use the SSMI as a basis of recording Inventories expense.

Lastly, to require the Inventory Committee to conduct a periodic physical inventory of supplies or property. The physical count of inventory items by type shall be conducted semestrally and reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI). This shall be submitted to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.

We have recommended that  the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Make a Restated Financial Statements to include the improper PPA for utilization of Inventories as expense for the current year. Adjust the statement of financial performance to reflect the corrected expense for comparability of financial statements across years.

Prepare a revised Reconciliation of SCBAA and Financial Performance to include the consumed inventory during CY 2023 attributable to previous appropriation as Accrual Basis recognizes expense when it is incurred not on the timing of payment.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

The Municipal Treasurer (MT) to send written confirmations to the payees holding the stale checks to ascertain whether or not these checks have been replaced. If replacements haven't been issued, demand the stale check for cancellation and proceed with issuing the replacement check. Following this, the MT should relay the results to the Municipal Accountant (MA);

The Municipal Accountant (MA) that upon receiving the results of confirmation from the MT, she should effect immediately the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to present fairly the balance of the Cash in Bank – LCCA account in the financial statements.

Below is the suggested pro-forma adjusting entry:

To revert-back the liability due to stale check:
Cash in Bank-LCCA	   xx
	Accounts Payable         xx

To issue a replacement check:
Accounts Payable        xx
   Cash in Bank-LCCA         xx

We have recommended that the Management carefully assess the maximum cash accountabilities of each Accountable Officers based on past transactions prior to application of fidelity bond in accordance with Sections 5.1 and 5.3 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 so that the interest of the Municipality is protected in case of loss or misapplication of public funds.

We have recommended for the Municipal Mayor to enjoin the municipal officials to refrain from paying registration fee more than the allowable limit provided in the NBC No. 563 for seminars/conference/trainings conducted by NGOs or Private Institutions. Excess from allowable limit shall be at the expense of the participants.
observations.reason: (Moot and academic due to policy updates)

observations.managementAction: The Management made an adjustment entry to correct the misclassification.

Demand letters were sent to Punong Barangays which have jurisdiction over the concerned individuals thru a registered mail which were duly received by the PB’s. 

The MSWDO is in close coordination with DSWD FO –I on next steps to be taken after sending the demand letters.
The Management submitted all the test results required by the COA-TAS to the Audit Team. They also require the test results as a requirement for release of final payment of any project.
The Management implemented the reversion of unexpended balances of MOOE and proper classification of budgetary items as continuing appropriation.
The Management discontinued the granting of said allowance effective June 2023
The Management submitted the AAPSI to the relevant government agencies and uploaded it to their website.
Management ceased charging meals to the Extraordinary and Miscellaneous account and instructed department heads to use their RATA for this purpose.
The Management discontinued the hiring of consultants effective July 2023.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Accountant to regularly review and analyze the affected accounts which have abnormal balances and undertake corrective measures to come up with the accurate balances of the accounts. This is to ensure that the financial statements are fairly presented.
observations.managementAction: On-going reconciliation and analysis of the said accounts is currently being done by the accounting office.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no. 2 page 31 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipality direct the office responsible to demand a refund of the amount received by the remaining 97 ineligible SAP beneficiaries totaling to ₱533,500.00 and cause the immediate return the unrefunded balance to the DSWD Field Office No. I, San Fernando City, La Union.
observations.managementAction: The MSWD Officer had already sent demand letters to the ineligible beneficiaries with the assistance of the Punong Barangay of the concerned barangays.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no.11 page 49 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the officials concerned to undertake measures to adhere with the COA Circular 2020-006 re: Guidelines and Procedures in the Conduct of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE), Recognition of PPE Items Found at Station, and Disposition for Non-existing/ Missing PPE Items, for the One Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies to come up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount due and collectible based on the RPTAR/Taxpayer's index card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in the setting up of receivables for the real property and special education taxes for a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries for the total unexpended balance of LGUs share from tobacco excise tax and
The use of appropriate account for transactions being recorded based on accounting rules and regulations issued by competent authorities to avoid misstatements of accounts and ultimately achieve a fairly presented and reliable set of financial statements.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace and review the transactions pertaining to the discrepancy and immediately prepare the necessary adjusting entry/ies to reconcile the reciprocal accounts.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting journal entry was already made for the unreconciled reciprocal accounts.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the above-cited transactions to its appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment account and prepare the proper journal entries to adjust the affected account balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Reclassification of accounts was already made and a copy of JEV was submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above-mentioned observations, we have recommended to the Management the following:

Strengthen its control mechanism to reconcile the records pertaining to the over-remittance and under-remittance of Inter-Agency Payables.

Regularly comply with the law particularly on the remittance of statutory liabilities to avoid interest, penalties and surcharges for not remitting the full amount withheld from employees, suppliers and contractors.
observations.managementAction: On-going tracing of prior year’s remittances for the reconciliation of inter-agency payables.
observations.reason:
Not Implemented.

The Audit Team acknowledged the effort of the management for the timely remittance of its statutory obligations however, deficiencies were noted hence, included in this year findings.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG) thoroughly evaluate the bids particularly on the calculation of bids (footings and cross footings) to readily identify errors or discrepancies resulting to possible overstatement which is among the basis for disqualification of bid. 

We have also recommended that Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG) to strictly adhere with the above-mentioned government procurement law by ensuring fair and transparency in all procurement activities.

We have recommended to the management the following: 
 
Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the copy of contract agreement and pertinent supporting documents of the above-mentioned project.

The BAC through the BAC Secretariat ensure the submission of the contracts, P.O.s and its supporting documents within five (5) working days from the execution of contracts as required under COA Circular No.2009-001 for a systematic and effective review process to come up with timely, relevant audit results.
observations.managementAction: The BAC has already submitted the approved contract and other related bidding documents.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management strictly adhere with COA Circular No. 98-002 and memorandum from the Office of Solicitor General in contracting services of a private lawyer.

We have also recommended that Management discontinue further payment of honoraria of the legal consultants unless a written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General as well as the written concurrence of the Commission on Audit, is secured to avoid suspension in audit.

For the above observations, we have recommended the following to management:

To prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant plantilla positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, projects and activities of the Municipality by hiring qualified applicants or by promoting non-permanent deserving personnel to permanent positions to lessen the need to hire job order/contractual workers.

To require the Chief Administrative Officer to adopt innovative ways to attract applicants for the vacant positions through a wider reach of publication and posting process.

To evaluate the reasons for the increase in the number of job order personnel and contractual workers and the need to hire such personnel especially those performing clerical and administrative functions.

We have recommended that Management to direct the Municipal Engineer the following:

For similar future projects, conduct effective and efficient planning through project site inspection by technical personnel to avoid delay and adjustments in the plan and implementation of project,

Regular monitoring in the implementation of succeeding projects to ensure completion thereof within the target completion dates to achieve utmost benefits for the public.
observations.managementAction: The said infrastructure project was already completed on October 14, 2021.
observations.reason:
Not Implemented.

There are still some projects that were not completed within the target completion date.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above-mentioned deficiencies/lapses, we recommend the following:

Require the concerned offices to conduct an investigation on the above stated project and submit to the Municipal Mayor the results of the investigation for appropriate action,

Require the Municipal Engineer to conduct regular monitoring in the implementation of succeeding projects to ensure completion thereof within the target completion dates.
observations.managementAction: Contract between the contractor and the LGU for the said infrastructure project was already terminated.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above-mentioned observations, we have recommended the following:

Management immediately direct the concerned offices to submit the required monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor copy furnished to the OCD and DILG and

Concerned officials to ensure the regular and timely submission of the said report in compliance with the above-mentioned laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Submission of Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund is already on a monthly basis.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the inventory committee should complete the conduct of inventory and submit duly reconciled required report on physical count of all the property, plant and equipment of the municipality as prescribed under Section 124 of the NGAS, Volume I of the LGU to ascertain the accuracy and validity of the  presented  PPE amount in the financial statement.
observations.reason: The inventory committee has yet to conduct complete physical inventory due to pandemic.

observations.managementAction: According to management, recorded amounts of Road Networks on the LGU books based on journals were already identified by the accounting office. However, details as to road components, project names and locations were incomplete because documents on file especially those pertaining to year 2020 backwards were incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation for the Acting Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the affected accounts which have abnormal balances and undertake corrective measures to come up with the correct balances of the accounts. This is to ensure that the financial statements are fairly presented.
observations.reason:
Despite the effort done by the accounting office in the reconciliation of the affected accounts, the same observation was noted in audit.

Audit Team issued AOM No. 2021-008 dated February 24, 2021 reiterating the audit observation and recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended therefore that Management institute the following control measures:

Regular reconciliation of records pertaining to the under and over remittance of premium contributions;

Maintenance of employees' individual ledger cards to monitor the deductions of each employee; and

PhilHealth premium contributions deducted from employees must be remitted within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: The Audit Team acknowledged the effort of the management for the timely remittance of Philhealth contributions, however deficiencies were noted hence, included in this year findings.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and Acting Municipal Accountant to remit all PAG-IBIG contributions especially those pertaining to prior years to avoid delay in the processing of members'/employees' claims and prevent possible forfeiture of the benefits due them. 

Likewise, we have recommended regular review/analysis of the details of the subsidiary ledger balances of Due to Pag-IBIG account to determine the reason for the discrepancies and effect the necessary adjustment/s thereof.
observations.reason: The Audit Team acknowledged the effort of the management for the timely remittance of Pag-Ibig contributions and loan amortizations, however deficiencies were noted hence, included in this year findings.

The Audit Team acknowledged the effort of the management for the timely remittance of GSIS contributions and loan amortizations, however deficiencies were noted hence, included in this year findings.

Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Members of the Sangguniang Bayan discontinue the practice of granting financial assistance/donations to their constituents as this is not among their mandated duties and functions under Section 447(a) of R.A. No. 7160. Any financial assistance/grants must be coursed through the proper department of the municipality for evaluation or assessment.

To address the foregoing audit issues raised, it was recommended that:

The LGU consider rescinding the contract and subject the availment of services for waste disposal to bidding in the compliance with RA 9184.

Requires the individual referred to as the "Second Party" in the MOA to issue Official Receipts for all payments to already rendered services for the waste disposal of the LGU, otherwise all payments will be considered irregular, therefore subject to disallowance in pursuant to COA Circular 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

LGU establish its control mechanism in order that only actual weights of garbage disposal will be paid & control mechanism should include the prevention of connivance between the LGUs employees with the Urdaneta Waste Management. This control mechanism would include an affidavit to be made by the drivers certifying the actual weights of garbage delivered. If possible the LGU can consider providing its own weighing scale
observations.reason: The management did not rescinded its contract with Urdaneta Waste Management for CY 2020, however a cease and desist order dated March 23, 2021 was issued by DENR to the said entity in view of non-compliance with RA No. 9003.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipal officials concerned should endeavor to implement the programs/projects as listed in the Annual Investment Program to the fullest possible to give the townspeople the maximum benefits they could derived and to strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.reason:
Validation of government PPAs showed that some projects funded under the 20% development fund CY 2019 were still not completed or remained unimplemented.

Such deficiencies were included in AOM No.2021-014 dated March 19, 2021.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the above-cited transactions to its appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment account and prepare the proper journal entries to adjust the affected account balances in the financial statements.

Strengthen its control mechanism to reconcile the records pertaining to the over-remittance and under-remittance of Inter-Agency Payables.
Regularly comply with the law particularly on the remittance of statutory liabilities to avoid interest, penalties and surcharges for not remitting the full amount withheld from employees, suppliers and contractors.

We have recommended that the Municipality direct the office responsible to demand a refund of the amount received by the remaining 97 ineligible SAP beneficiaries totaling to P533,500.00 and cause the immediate return the unrefunded balance to the DSWD Field Office No. I, San Fernando City, La Union.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG) thoroughly evaluate the bids particularly on the calculation of bids (footings and cross footings) to readily identify errors or discrepancies resulting to possible overstatement which is among the basis for disqualification of bid.
We have also recommended that Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG) to strictly adhere with the above-mentioned government procurement law by ensuring fair and transparency in all procurement activities.

We have recommended to the management the following:
Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the copy of contract agreement and pertinent supporting documents of the above-mentioned project.
The BAC through the BAC Secretariat ensure the submission of the contracts, P.O.s and its supporting documents within five (5) working days from the execution of contracts as required under COA Circular No.2009-001 for a systematic and effective review process to come up with timely, relevant audit results.

We have recommended that Management strictly adhere with COA Circular No. 98-002 and memorandum from the Office of Solicitor General in contracting services of a private lawyer.
We have also recommended that Management discontinue further payment of honoraria of the legal consultants unless a written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General as well as the written concurrence of the Commission on Audit, is secured to avoid suspension in audit.

To prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant plantilla positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, projects and activities of the Municipality by hiring qualified applicants or by promoting non-permanent deserving personnel to permanent positions to lessen the need to hire job order/contractual workers.
To require the Chief Administrative Officer to adopt innovative ways to attract applicants for the vacant positions through a wider reach of publication and posting process.
To evaluate the reasons for the increase in the number of job order personnel and contractual workers and the need to hire such personnel especially those performing clerical and administrative functions.

We have recommended that Management to direct the Municipal Engineer the following:
For similar future projects, conduct effective and efficient planning through project site inspection by technical personnel to avoid delay and adjustments in the plan and implementation of project,
Regular monitoring in the implementation of succeeding projects to ensure completion thereof within the target completion dates to achieve utmost benefits for the public.

Require the concerned offices to conduct an investigation on the above stated project and submit to the Municipal Mayor the results of the investigation for appropriate action,
Require the Municipal Engineer to conduct regular monitoring in the implementation of succeeding projects to ensure completion thereof within the target completion dates.

In view of the above-mentioned observations, we have recommended the following:
Management immediately direct the concerned offices to submit the required monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor copy furnished to the OCD and DILG and
Concerned officials to ensure the regular and timely submission of the said report in compliance with the above-mentioned laws, rules and regulations.

In line with the above-mentioned observations, we have recommended the following:

Strictly observe the procedure provided in Section 50 of the NGAS Manual by re-classifying the completed projects from the CIP account to their appropriate asset account. 

Make the necessary adjusting journal entries to transfer the amount of P7,883,651.97 to the appropriate asset account.

Verify the cause of inconsistencies of the amount of three (3) completed projects previously dropped from the CIP account and effect necessary adjusting journal entries, if any.
observations.managementAction: Management already submitted journal entry vouchers for the adjustments of CIP account to its appropriate PPE account.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of inventories and instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in the books to bring the balance of the inventory account to its correct balance.

With the above observations we have recommended the following:

For Management to direct the concerned employee/s to immediately refund the amount of P1,520.09. 

For the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit intact all the PhilHealth contributions withheld within the prescribed period to avoid incurrence of unnecessary charges and liabilities that maybe imposed by PHIC for delayed remittance.
observations.reason: The Audit Team acknowledged the effort of the management for the timely remittance of Philhealth contributions, however deficiencies were noted hence, included in this year findings.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant to discontinue journalizing payment of solid waste as Rent Expense. We further have recommended that the succeeding transactions be properly classified and recorded to its appropriate account, Environment/Sanitary Services in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 to achieve fair presentation of account balances in the FS.
observations.reason: Per review of the submitted FS for CY 2020, the account Environment/Sanitary services was used in recording the payment of solid waste.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer closely monitor the balances of leave credits of the officials and employees to ensure that at least 5 days is retained after monetization pursuant to Section 22 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave.  In cases of monetization of more than 50% of accumulated leave credits, officials and employees must submit additional documentary requirements to support their request for monetization provided in Section 5.14 of COA Circular 2012-001.

To strengthen the internal control system in monitoring the service rendered and accomplishments of the Job Order Workers and casual/contractual personnel, we have recommended the following:

That the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) require the submission of monthly accomplishment report of job order workers and casual/contractual personnel duly signed by an official concerned. The same must be submitted to the Accounting Office to support the payment of wages.

That the HRMO submit the approved contract or its equivalent of job orders to COA to validate the propriety of the payment of wages to them.

Lastly, provide the COA auditors the complete list of all job orders, casual and contractual wherein included in the list the nature of employment, office of assignment, designation and duration of service to be rendered by the personnel for validation purposes.

We have recommended that Management require from the officials and employees concerned to submit the required supporting documents in compliance with the above-cited COA Circular. Succeeding transactions on repairs and maintenance must be supported with pre and post-repair inspection reports in order to establish the validity of the claims.
observations.managementAction: Documentary requirements such as pre-repair inspection and post-repair inspection reports were already submitted by the Management in compliance with audit observation.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant or her staff require the concerned officials to secure and submit distribution list or acknowledgement receipt to support the disbursement. Furthermore, all supporting documents attached to every paid Disbursement Voucher should be original copies.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted list of recipients and items delivered to various barangays

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require that all pakyaw contracts be notarized to safeguard the interest of the Municipality. We have also recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant shall see to it that supporting documents attached to the disbursement vouchers are original copies.

To ensure that the internal control system is adequate, we have recommended the following:

That the Acting Municipal Accountant to be more diligent in the review of the supporting documents before endorsing them for payment,

That Management require the persons responsible in altering the supporting documents to submit written explanation on why the alterations occurred.

Require the Bids and Awards Committee to conduct quotations from at least 3 bonafide suppliers in compliance with the existing procurement law.

Furthermore on the payments of any claims, ensure that the claims are received only by the right or authorized person/payee.
observations.managementAction: According to the Management, the payment issued to the claimant under check no.1502378 dated April 16, 2019 was lost by the payee. The payee however, waived her right to request for replacement of the check and donated the amount due to her to the LGU Pozorrubio.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer exert utmost effort to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts in compliance with the cited guidelines, policies and auditing rules and regulations to avoid undue delays in the conduct of post audit.
observations.reason: Verification of records and transmittal of accounts and documents revealed that management was able to submit the required reports within the prescribed timeframe.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the concerned officials to exert utmost efforts to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts. This is to avoid undue delay from timely conduct of audit and rendering of the required reports in compliance with the cited existing laws, rules and regulations. Reports when submitted on time are useful information that can aid management in decision making.
observations.reason: Verification of records and transmittal of accounts and documents revealed that management was able to submit the required reports within the prescribed timeframe.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that management strictly comply with the General Insurance Law as amended COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992 by conducting an annual inventory of all the insurable properties of the LGU. Subsequently prepare and submit a report verified by COA to the GSIS on or before October 31 of the ensuing year.

We have further reiterated that adequate budget for payment of the required insurance premiums be provided and paid to the GSIS.

observations.recommendation:

Remit intact all withheld taxes to the Bureau of Internal Revenue in compliance to Revenue Regulations 2-98 and provisions of RA 8424 to avoid paying penalties, surcharges and interest arising from violations. Failure to do so may result to punishment as specified under Section 272 of the said Act.

Recommendation:

Regular reconciliation of records pertaining to the under and over remittance of premium contributions;
Maintenance of employees' individual ledger cards to monitor the deductions of each employee; and
3.	PhilHealth premium contributions deducted from employees must be remitted within the prescribed period.

Recommendation:

2. 	For the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit intact all the PhilHealth contributions withheld within the prescribed period to avoid incurrence of unnecessary charges and liabilities that maybe imposed by PHIC for delayed remittance.

Likewise, we have recommended regular review/analysis of the details of the subsidiary ledger balances of Due to PAG-IBIG account to determine the reason for the discrepancies and effect the necessary adjustment/s thereof.

Recommendation:
In view of the foregoing deficiencies, we have recommended to Management the following:
To reconcile the records pertaining to the unremitted contributions and loan repayments and to effect immediately the remittance of long overdue accounts, if any;
2.	To remit intact all the GSIS contributions and loan amortizations on or before the 10th day of the following month to ensure that officials and employees enjoy the GSIS benefits to the maximum and as well as to avoid incurrence of penalties.

No. 7160. Any financial assistance/grants must be coursed through the proper department of the municipality for evaluation or assessment.

Recommendation:
We have reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that management strictly comply with the General Insurance Law as amended COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992 by conducting an annual inventory of all the insurable properties of the LGU. Subsequently prepare and submit a report verified by COA to the GSIS on or before October 31 of the ensuing year.
We have further reiterated that adequate budget for payment of the required insurance premiums be provided and paid to the GSIS.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the municipal officials concerned should endeavor to implement the programs/projects as listed in the Annual Investment Program to the fullest possible to give the townspeople the maximum benefits they could derived and to strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1.

Recommendation:
We have recommended for Management to institute the following control measures:
Instruct the concerned offices to reconcile the records pertaining to unremitted taxes withheld and to enforce immediately the remittance of overdue accounts, if any.
2.	Remit intact all withheld taxes to the Bureau of Internal Revenue in compliance to Revenue Regulations 2-98 and provisions of RA 8424 to avoid paying penalties, surcharges and interest arising from violations. Failure to do so may result to punishment as specified under Section 272 of the said Act.

We recommended the Management to direct the Municipal Treasury to conduct its physical inventory and to submit immediately the inventory report in compliance with Section 124 Volume I of NGAS Manual. Also, please require the Municipal Treasury to maintain its property cards as required under the above provision of the NGAS Manual.

	We further recommended for the Accounting Office to prepare its property ledger cards to facilitate the reconciliation of physical inventory balances with that of the Municipal Treasury Office records.
observations.reason: Management has yet to fully implement the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the affected accounts which have abnormal balances and undertake corrective measures to come up with the correct balances of the accounts to ensure that the financial statements are fairly presented.
observations.reason: Abnormal balance is still reflected in the FS

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of inventories and instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in the books to bring the balance of the inventory account to the correct balance.

We recommended that Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit the required documents to facilitate review as to compliance with auditorial and technical requirements. Likewise, submit the certified true copy of Transfer Certificate of Title and Tax Declaration in the of LGU and Deed of Donation duly registered in the Registry of Deeds in compliance with the Memorandum of Agreement.

           We further recommended that the Technical Working Group (TWG) to exercise due diligence in the post-qualification of the bids to determine whether the bidder concerned complies with and are responsive to all requirements and conditions specified in the bidding documents and/or in the IRR.

          Lastly, we recommended that the BAC and TWG to submit written explanation on the lapses/deficiencies enumerated above.
observations.reason: Required documents were not yet completely submitted

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above deficiencies, we recommended for the Management the following:

          Submit the above cited lacking supporting documents on subject disbursements for further evaluation pursuant to PD No.1445,

         Enforce strictly the submission of Purchase Orders (POs) and all supporting documents within five (5) days from its issuance or execution pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001,

           Ensure that the required number of feeding days for the program is completed to attain the objective for which the fund was released.
observations.reason: Required documents were not yet submitted

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create duly designated Inspectorate Committee with members capable of handling inspection of supplies, materials, equipment and other deliveries to uphold the principles of internal control relative to the check and balance in the procurement process.

We recommended that a demand of refund must be made from those officials and employees who have not yet make a refund of their PBB in full or in part. The non-refund of the unauthorized expenditure will now be aptly dealt with through the issuance of a Notice of Disallowance (ND).
observations.reason: Refund by the employees is on-going

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and ensure proper planning so that all its programmed development projects during the year will be implemented for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.reason: Programs/Projects is yet to be fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System duly created prepare and submit the GAD Accomplishment Report (AR) for proper monitoring and evaluation of accomplishments as well as identifying the major constraints or problems encountered.

We recommended that control must be infused in the releasing of the checks or payments so that receipts/invoices issued by the suppliers will be stringently scrutinized as to its validity and propriety.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Management to direct the Municipal Treasury to conduct its physical inventory and to submit immediately the inventory report in compliance with Section 124 Volume I of NGAS Manual. Also, please require the Municipal Treasury to maintain its property cards as required under the above provision of the NGAS Manual.
We further recommended for the Accounting Office to prepare its property ledger cards to facilitate the reconciliation of physical inventory balances with that of the Municipal Treasury Office records.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Mayor said that an Inventory Committee is already created to conduct a physical inventory of the properties of the Municipality. He also stated that Management is still waiting for the designation of the head of the Inventory Team who will lead the inventory of properties. Further, the Municipal Treasurer cited that the conduct of physical inventory of the equipments is on-going.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the affected accounts which have abnormal balances and undertake corrective measures to come up with the correct balances of the accounts to ensure that the financial statements are fairly presented.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of inventories and instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in the books to bring the balance of the inventory account to the correct balance.
The Municipal Engineer during the exit conference commented that he will provide an updated list of the subject construction materials and will furnish to the Accounting Office to reconcile the balance on hand of the said inventory item.

Recommendation:
With all the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Management to:
Instruct the BAC Secretariat to carefully observe the proper identification of the contract to be bid and the categorization of the items in the Invitation to Bid in posting at the PhilGEPS;
Likewise, avoid inclusion of items in the Purchase Request (PR) which incongruous to each other to achieve the most advantageous price and quality in favor of the LGU.
Submit a written explanation on the noted inconsistencies and deficiencies noted on the above transaction;
Create Inspection Team with members capable of handling inspection of supplies and materials for check and balance; and
Record all purchases on drugs and medicines in the books using the Perpetual Inventory Method.
In the exit conference, the representative of the RHU acknowledged the audit team’s recommendation and committed to comply.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit the required documents to facilitate review as to compliance with auditorial and technical requirements. Likewise, submit the certified true copy of Transfer Certificate of Title and Tax Declaration in the of LGU and Deed of Donation duly registered in the Registry of Deeds in compliance with the Memorandum of Agreement.
We further recommended that the Technical Working Group (TWG) to exercise due diligence in the post-qualification of the bids to determine whether the bidder concerned complies with and are responsive to all requirements and conditions specified in the bidding documents and/or in the IRR.
Lastly, we recommended that the BAC and TWG to submit written explanation on the lapses/deficiencies enumerated above.
During the conduct of exit conference, the MPDC being the BAC Chairman conveyed to the Audit Team that the delayed submission/compliance of the documents was due to change in administration. He also mentioned that related documents such as Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) is now being processed to the Registry of Deeds.

Recommendation:
In view of the above deficiencies, we recommended for the Management the following:
Submit the above cited lacking supporting documents on subject disbursements for further evaluation pursuant to PD No.1445,
Enforce strictly the submission of Purchase Orders (POs) and all supporting documents within five (5) days from its issuance or execution pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001,
Ensure that the required number of feeding days for the program is completed to attain the objective for which the fund was released.
In the exit conference, the MPDC mentioned that according to the GAD Focal person, Management has already submitted the GAD Accomplishment Report within the deadline following the new joint memorandum circular issued by the concerned government agencies.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management create duly designated Inspectorate Committee with members capable of handling inspection of supplies, materials, equipment and other deliveries to uphold the principles of internal control relative to the check and balance in the procurement process.
The Municipal Mayor during the exit conference is amenable to our audit recommendation by creating two (2) separate inspection team for regular deliveries such as goods and another team who will be assigned to inspect for infrastructure projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended that a demand of refund must be made from those officials and employees who have not yet make a refund of their PBB in full or in part. The non-refund of the unauthorized expenditure will now be aptly dealt with through the issuance of a Notice of Disallowance (ND).
The Local Chief Executive during the exit conference directed the concerned Department Heads to update the list of officials and employees with unsettled PBB and the same to have reconciliation with the accounting office. In addition, he also stated that Management will make another memorandum for those officials and employees who have not yet refunded their PBB received.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and ensure proper planning so that all its programmed development projects during the year will be implemented for the benefit of the constituents.
The Municipal Engineer admitted that there was indeed a backlog on the implementation of the developmental projects because they prioritized the implementation on those projects funded by the DILG. However, he also explained that for the first quarter of the year 2019, the concerned offices was able to cope up the project implementation and was able to reduce its backlog.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System duly created prepare and submit the GAD Accomplishment Report (AR) for proper monitoring and evaluation of accomplishments as well as identifying the major constraints or problems encountered.
In the exit conference, the MPDC mentioned that according to the GAD Focal person, Management has already submitted the GAD Accomplishment Report within the deadline following the new joint memorandum circular issued by the concerned government agencies.

Recommendation:
We recommended that control must be infused in the releasing of the checks or payments so that receipts/invoices issued by the suppliers will be stringently scrutinized as to its validity and propriety.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Treasurer asserted that she already informed the concerned suppliers to replace the officials receipts previously issued to the LGU Pozorrubio and provide new and valid official receipt. Further, the Municipal Treasurer favorably noted our audit recommendation.

I.    We recommended the Local Chief Executive to initiate move by creating Inventory Committee that will conduct and complete the physical count of all properties of the municipality to know the existence and condition of these properties.

We also recommended the preparation and submission to the Auditor the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in compliance with Section 124 of the MNGAS and in consonance with Section 156 of COA Circular 92-386 which will be used to reconcile with the Property Cards maintained by the Treasurer’s Office and Property Ledger Cards maintained by the Accounting Office.

II. Strictly adhere to the pertinent sections of COA 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances in consonance with COA circular 2012-001 or the Revised Guidelines and Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions to check the completeness and propriety of the documents submitted for liquidation to avoid suspension in audit.

Observe rules and regulations of Executive Order no. 298 in availing traveling 	expenses and correct computation of traveling allowances and per diems.
III. We recommended that Management ensure adherence to the above-cited provisions of DBM Budget Circular. Claims from honoraria should not exceed the actual collections.

IV. We recommended that Management ensure that all claims are supported with complete documentary requirements to support the validity, propriety and legality of all disbursements.	
Further, lacking documents must be submitted to avoid suspension of the transaction in audit.

V. We recommended that Management refrain from the practice of paying and charging prior year expenses to current year appropriation for proper matching of income and expenditures to be able to reflect the correct balance of net income for a particular period. This is in adherence with the above-quoted provisions of R.A. No.7160 and PD 1445.
Expenses of the previous year paid on the current year should be recorded in the books with the following entry:
On the year expense was incurred
Dr. 	Expense Account		xxx
			Cr.	Accounts Payable		xxx 
	On the year expense was paid
Dr. 	Accounts Payable		xxx
			Cr.	Cash		xxx

VI. For all the foregoing observations, we recommended the following:
That Management to ensure payment of salaries and wages based on the rates provided in the contract of service;

That all payrolls should be reviewed and recomputed by the HRMO/Chief Administrative Officer prior to processing of the payroll for cash advance.

That Management require the liable person to restitute/refund the excess payment on salaries/wages received by Ferdinand D. Rasid for the period January 16-31, 2017.

VII.We recommended that Management to prepare the payrolls using the prescribed format pursuant to Section 34 and Section 35 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System, Volume II.
Likewise, we recommended that the Immediate Supervisor certify in the monthly payrolls to ensure correctness of actual services rendered by employees under his direct supervision.

VIII.We recommended submission of above-listed requirements to avoid possible suspension of the transaction in audit. 

We also recommended that Management strictly comply with the documentary requirements set forth in the COA Circular 2012-001 as basis for payment of BAC honoraria.

IX. We recommended that the Management avoid reimbursement as a mode of procurement and adopt public bidding or other alternative modes of procurement provided under RA 9184, in order to promote transparency and to avoid possible source of irregularities.

We also recommended that regular purchase of materials, supplies and equipments by the Municipality should be made through check disbursement direct to the supplier/creditor. 

Likewise, compliance with BIR regulations should be adhered to by the Municipal Accounting Office on withholding of taxes.

We further recommended that Management refrain from specifying the brand names in the Purchase Requests pursuant to Section 18 of the revised IRR of R.A. 9184 to get the widest range of bid proposals.

X. We recommended that Management ensure the submission of the Purchase Orders (P.O.s) and its supporting documents within five (5) working days from  issuance pursuant to the COA Circular No.2009-001 in order for the audit team to conduct timely review of the documents submitted.
	Likewise, we recommended that Municipal officials and employees concerned shall furnish the Auditor copies of approved Purchase Requests (PRs) for review and evaluation.
XI. We recommended that Bids and Awards Committee to submit the required documents in order to facilitate review as to compliance with legal and auditorial requirements.

XII. We recommended that the municipal officials concerned should to the fullest extent possible, ensure that all programs/ projects listed in its Annual Investment Program be implemented to give the municipal constituents the maximum benefits that could be derived and to adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.reason: Manage-ment has yet to act on this finding

observations.recommendation: XIII.We recommended that GAD Plan and Budget as well as the Annual Accomplishment Report must be submitted to the concerned identified agencies on the date prescribed for review and monitoring.  Likewise, full implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget is also recommended in order to realize the intended result and extending the expected benefits to beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Manage-ment has yet to act on this finding

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to initiate move by creating Inventory Committee that will conduct and complete the physical count of all properties of the municipality to know the existence and condition of these properties.
We also recommended the preparation and submission to the Auditor the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in compliance with Section 124 of the MNGAS and in consonance with Section 156 of COA Circular 92-386 which will be used to reconcile with the Property Cards maintained by the Treasurer’s Office and Property Ledger Cards maintained by the Accounting Office.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the agency should:
Strictly adhere to the pertinent sections of COA 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances in consonance with COA circular 2012-001 or the Revised Guidelines and Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions to check the completeness and propriety of the documents submitted for liquidation to avoid suspension in audit.
Observe rules and regulations of Executive Order no. 298 in availing traveling 	expenses and correct computation of traveling allowances and per diems.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management ensure adherence to the above-cited provisions of DBM Budget Circular. Claims from honoraria should not exceed the actual collections

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management ensure that all claims are supported with complete documentary requirements to support the validity, propriety and legality of all disbursements.
Further, lacking documents must be submitted to avoid suspension of the transaction in audit.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management refrain from the practice of paying and charging prior year expenses to current year appropriation for proper matching of income and expenditures to be able to reflect the correct balance of net income for a particular period. This is in adherence with the above-quoted provisions of R.A. No.7160 and PD 1445.
Expenses of the previous year paid on the current year should be recorded in the books with the following entry:
On the year expense was incurred
Dr.	Expense Account		xxx
Cr.	Accounts Payable		xxx
On the year expense was paid
Dr.	Accounts Payable		xxx
Cr.	Cash			xxx

Recommendation:
For all the foregoing observations, we recommended the following:
That Management to ensure payment of salaries and wages based on the rates provided in the contract of service;
That all payrolls should be reviewed and recomputed by the HRMO/Chief Administrative Officer prior to processing of the payroll for cash advance.
That Management require the liable person to restitute/refund the excess payment on salaries/wages received by Ferdinand D. Rasid for the period January 16-31, 2017.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management to prepare the payrolls using the prescribed format pursuant to Section 34 and Section 35 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System, Volume II.
Likewise, we recommended that the Immediate Supervisor certify in the monthly payrolls to ensure correctness of actual services rendered by employees under his direct supervision.

Recommendation:
We recommended submission of above-listed requirements to avoid possible suspension of the transaction in audit.
2. 	We also recommended that Management strictly comply with the documentary requirements set forth in the COA Circular 2012-001 as basis for payment of BAC honoraria.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management avoid reimbursement as a mode of procurement and adopt public bidding or other alternative modes of procurement provided under RA 9184, in order to promote transparency and to avoid possible source of irregularities.
We also recommended that regular purchase of materials, supplies and equipments by the Municipality should be made through check disbursement direct to the supplier/creditor.
Likewise, compliance with BIR regulations should be adhered to by the Municipal Accounting Office on withholding of taxes.
We further recommended that Management refrain from specifying the brand names in the Purchase Requests pursuant to Section 18 of the revised IRR of R.A. 9184 to get the widest range of bid proposals.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management ensure the submission of the Purchase Orders (P.O.s) and its supporting documents within five (5) working days from  issuance pursuant to the COA Circular No.2009-001 in order for the audit team to conduct timely review of the documents submitted.
Likewise, we recommended that Municipal officials and employees concerned shall furnish the Auditor copies of approved Purchase Requests (PRs) for review and evaluation.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Bids and Awards Committee to submit the required documents in order to facilitate review as to compliance with legal and auditorial requirements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the municipal officials concerned should to the fullest extent possible, ensure that all programs/ projects listed in its Annual Investment Program be implemented to give the municipal constituents the maximum benefits that could be derived and to adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

Recommendation:
We recommended that GAD Plan and Budget as well as the Annual Accomplishment Report must be submitted to the concerned identified agencies on the date prescribed for review and monitoring.  Likewise, full implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget is also recommended in order to realize the intended result and extending the expected benefits to beneficiaries.

For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we have recommended the following:

Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December of every year. The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the PPE and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pin point responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs.

Maintain/update PPE ledger cards, Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE), Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) and secure property tags for proper identification, control and monitoring of these PPEs.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | On-going verification and updating of PPE accounts and property tags.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002. Likewise, to consistently monitor the granting, utilization as well as check the propriety, validity and completeness of the supporting documents needed in the liquidation of these cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | The Municipal Accountant commits to observe the provisions of COA Circular 97-002.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the management to comply with Section 449, Volume I of GAAM to facilitate the transfer/titling of ownership of those remaining untitled lots to avoid ownership problems in the future. Also, to secure owner’s duplicate copy of the four (4) lots reported to have their respective TCTs.

Likewise, for the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers specifically the Real Property Ledger Card in recording the land to know the details of these lots and substantiate the same as well as to reconcile the amount recorded in the ledger card with the amount presented in the Financial Statement.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | Municipal Accountant has already maintained Real Property Ledger cards.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant to reclassify and record the collections from sale of bidding documents to the Trust Fund account.

observations.reason: Verified subsidiary ledger of Accounts Payable-Others-BAC Funds under General Fund and found that no transfer was made to Trust Fund as of year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management strictly comply with the General Insurance Law as amended COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992 by conducting an annual inventory of all the insurable properties of the LGU. Subsequently prepare and submit a report verified by COA to the GSIS on or before October 31 of the ensuing year.

We have further recommended that adequate budget for payment of the required insurance premiums be provided and paid to the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that officials concerned consistently adhere to policies on the payment of RATA as set forth in the Local Budget Circular No. 103 by requiring actual performance of respective functions as condition precedent in the claim of RATA.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | Payment of RATA is already in compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipality continue abide with the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund as enunciated in the DILG and DBM Joint Circular 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011. Full implementation should be aimed at to derive maximum benefits out of the fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | Management is yet to reduce its backlog in the implementation of its project for 2017.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding no.__ of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the GAD Focal Point System duly created prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report (AR) for proper monitoring and evaluation of accomplishments as well as identifying the major constraints or problems encountered.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | GAD Accomplishment Report was submitted to the office of DILG- Pozorrubio on March 9, 2016 and DILG Cluster Binalonan and COA Office on March 16, 2017.

observations.recommendation:
For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we have recommended the following:
Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December of every year. The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the PPE and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pin point responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs.
Maintain/update PPE ledger cards, Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE), Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS)and secure property tags for proper identification, control and monitoring of these PPEs.

We have recommendedthat the Municipal Accountant continue to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002. Likewise, to consistently monitor the granting, utilization as well as check the propriety, validity and completeness of the supporting documents needed in the liquidation of these cash advances.

We have recommended to the management to comply with Section 449, Volume I of GAAM to facilitate the transfer/titling of ownership of those remaining untitled lots to avoid ownership problems in the future. Also, to secure owner’s duplicate copy of the four (4) lots reported to have their respective TCTs.
Likewise, for the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers specifically the Real Property Ledger Card in recording the land to know the details of these lots and substantiate the same as well as to reconcile the amount recorded in the ledger card with the amount presented in the Financial Statement.
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant to reclassify and record the collections from sale of bidding documents to the Trust Fund account.

We have recommended that management strictly comply with the General  Insurance Law as amended COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992 by conducting an annual inventory of all the insurable properties of the LGU. Subsequently prepare and submit a report verified by COA to the GSIS on or before October 31 of the ensuing year.
We have further recommended that adequate budget for payment of the required insurance premiums be provided and paid to the GSIS.

We have recommended that officials concerned consistently adhere to policies on the payment of RATA as set forth in the Local Budget Circular No. 103 by requiring actual performance of respective functions as condition precedent in the claim of RATA.

We have recommended that the municipality continue abide with the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund as enunciated in the DILG and DBM Joint Circular 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011. Full implementation should be aimed at to derive maximum benefits out of the fund.

We have recommended that the GAD Focal Point System duly created prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report (AR) for proper monitoring and evaluation of accomplishments as well as identifying the major constraints or problems encountered.

We have recommended that the inventory committee be created whose primary task is to conduct physical count of all the property, plant and equipment of the municipality and prepare a report on the result of the physical count. Subsequently, reconcile the report by effecting necessary adjustments on the reported valuation of the properties presented in the financial statements and submit the same to the office of the auditor as prescribed under Section 124 of the NGAS, Volume I of the LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Management has only submitted list of its Property, Plant and Equipment.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer clarify why the supplemental budget particularly that which pertains to PBB was allowed to be utilized even if approval for budget on PBB was deferred by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan. It is further recommended that the LGU apprise the team on actions taken to comply with the requirement of SP to approve grant of PBB to the LGUs municipal officers and employees.
observations.managementAction: Communications to the concerned agencies were submitted but compliance to SP requirement is yet to be obtained.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management consistently observe the provisions on the payment and charging of BAC and TWG Honoraria in compliance with DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3.

We have recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Point System-Technical Working Group (GFPS-TWG) to address the concerns of DILG as contained in their certification to ensure full compliance with the provisions of JMC No. 2013-01.

We have recommended that Management thru the GFPS-TWG to ensure full implementation of Programs, Plans and Activities to fully achieve the GAD mandate and to accord to the beneficiaries the intended benefits to be derived from the GAD-related activities.

We have recommended that Management continue with its thrust to fully implement plans, programs and activities in the LDRRM Plan to fully achieve the objectives of RA 10121.

We also have recommended for management for the creation of at least four (4) statutory DRRM positions who shall oversee operations of the DRRMOffice to uphold the purpose for which the DRRM Office was created and in compliance withSection 12 of RA 10121.

We have recommended the agency to continue to abide with the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% development fund as mentioned in the DILG and DBM Joint Circular 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
